pull.title: wallet: fix importdescriptors batch abort on timestamp error
pull.body:
Fixes #35181.

`importdescriptors` returns one result per request, but timestamp parsing
happened before the per-item result was created. A missing or invalid
timestamp could therefore throw a top-level RPC error and abort the batch
response after earlier requests may already have been applied.

Handle timestamp failures per item by adding a `{success: false, error: ...}`
result for the failing request and continuing with later requests.

Store parsed timestamps during the import loop so the rescan error path does
not need to parse failed requests again.

Tested:

```bash
python3 build/test/functional/test_runner.py wallet_importdescriptors.py --timeout-factor=3

Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first.

cACK

Will review the code soon.
I think this change makes sense. @achow101 is there a reason why we would want to throw and not store the error on each import?

Apart from that, there is a PR refactoring the `importdescriptors` code in #34861. I think it may make sense for you to try to add this patch on top of that new code. Or even maybe, if this gets a few concept ACKs I can cherry-pick your commit and add the changes in the refactor.

events.message:
wallet: return per-item error for bad timestamp in importdescriptors

importdescriptors is documented to return one result for each request.
Timestamp parsing happened before the per-item result was created, so a
missing or invalid timestamp threw a top-level RPC error and aborted the
batch. Earlier items could already have been applied, but the response
was lost.

Catch timestamp failures per item, append a failed result, and continue.
Store parsed timestamps so the rescan path does not re-parse failed
requests.

events.body:
Concept ACK

This looks good, thanks for picking this up!

One thing I noticed is that the error message is `Missing required timestamp field for key`, which I found a bit vague because it doesn't specify for which key. Do you think it would make sense for a follow-up to update `GetImportTimestamp` to change that error message to `Missing required timestamp field for key: <insert key>`?

I can pick that up if wanted

Thanks, agree the current message is vague.

I’d leave it unchanged here to keep this PR focused on changing timestamp failures from a top-level RPC error to a per-item result.

If you want to pick that up, I think replacing `key` with `import request` would be more accurate since the result array already identifies which request failed.
This will need a release note explaining the output change for the RPC.

events.message: doc: add release note for importdescriptors output change

events.body: Since #34861 is refactoring all of the the importdescriptors things,  I think we'll prefer to include any importdescriptors fixes in that PR to avoid conflicts.
